title: porophyllum ellipticum var. ruderale
intro: Porophyllum ellipticum var. ruderale, also known as Tree Marigold or Mexican Marigold, is a plant species native to Mexico and parts of the United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic properties. The health benefits of Tree Marigold include its ability to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin infections. Herbal preparations of Tree Marigold can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat a range of ailments.
